challenge_identified: Difficulties obtaining support from a reluctant experienced engineer
related_quote: I have one expert engineer on our team but he really is busy and very begrudgingly responds when asked things.

challenge_identified: Encouraging team to adopt agile vertical slicing practices
related_quote: How do I get my team to work in agile way and slice things vertically?

challenge_identified: Do you think it is the team members responsibility to continuously silently correct PMs weaknesses?
related_quote: Do you think it is the team members responsibility to continuously silently correct PMs weaknesses?

challenge_identified: Frustration with lack of acknowledgment of suggestions as Scrum Master
related_quote: I have made numerous suggestions (as the Scrum Master), I think four times, in the past year but it fell on deaf ears.

challenge_identified: Difficulties getting a developer who is resistant to change to commit to the new PM and programming framework.
related_quote: But I just can't seem to get this person on board by committing to our Sprint goals and work tools (i.e. Rails).

challenge_identified: Organization fails to deliver a Product Owner [...]. However, not having a product owner does slow us down and demoralizes the team in my opinion, because nobody seems to want to take responsibility for the final product.
related_quote: Organization fails to deliver a Product Owner [...].

challenge_identified: Scrum Masters struggle with managers who want updates on 'specific things' or 'better understanding', leading to confusion and lack of focus.
related_quote: The Scrum Master has tried mentioning that this is not a technical meeting, but the manager replies that he wants to know what's going on "with this specific thing" or "we need to understand this better" [...].

challenge_identified: Feeling overwhelmed and frustrated due to conflicting expectations regarding creative control and leadership responsibilities.
related_quote: Balance between sharing creative control and leading the project. [...] I don't want to be a project dictator but when we are in 'crunch time' and somebody goes off on a tangent with an idea and reports back that he doesn't think my solution is ideal, I find myself becoming aggravated.

challenge_identified: Silence and lack of participation in web scrum meetings lead to inefficiency and missed opportunities.
related_quote: While I don't worry too much about silence, from individual discussions I know there are things that are not being brought up, and I also feel we are not being very efficient where the meeting is longer than it should be due to the silence.

challenge_identified: Difficulties coordinating across different projects and departments while maintaining focus on individual tasks.
related_quote: A development workflow for a team of 3 developers that minimizes outside interruptions? [...] but where my team fails is when we are all working on separate projects and we get bombarded with input from many other departments.

challenge_identified: Inadequate soft skills training and lack of emphasis on communication skills among team members impact the ability to understand and meet customer requirements.
related_quote: Incorrect pronunciation leads to miscommunication which impacts customer confidence [...] problems for the team in understanding customer requirements [...] Team not successful in convincing clients when requirement creeps [...] Soft skills trainings are not usually conducted in small companies [...] Team do not give importance to communication skills and they believe it is not a part of their job

challenge_identified: She feels threatened by decisions made by the PM and resists refactoring code unless specifically asked.
related_quote: For some reason (possibly culturally as she's from another country) she takes any decisions made by the PM's personally though will back down when her manager asks her to do something [...] If I ask her to refactor some code to make it easier to manage, she will start into a tirade with any number of excuses as to why it needs to stay the way she wrote it. [...] If her manager asks her to make a similar change she does so without question.

challenge_identified: Difficulties dealing with arguments and defending past actions within a peer-to-peer setting
related_quote: Driving Changes on a peer-to-peer level [...] If I would be the leader, I would just say. "Stop it. Don't waste time for this report" [...]. But I am not the leader. I know, that if you ask "Why do you need this report?", then the others will find a lot of reasons why they think the report is needed. [...]. The more I ask into the direction like "stop it", the more the others will think they need to defend what they did in the past.

challenge_identified: Difficulties dealing with argumentative and uncooperative team members
related_quote: Every time I walk into their sprint planning session, I do not like what I see. [...] It's absolute silence and we can practically hear ourselves breathing. [...] The SM is the only person talking and tries to create tasks, one-by-one, for each story. [...] I try to encourage the PO and developers to ask questions, but I have developers who are very "passive" (not pro-active). [...] No developer will voluntarily request to be assigned a task (like I use to do and have seen done by so many of my peers). [...] They sit and almost wait to be assigned tasks (which was how it was before I introduced Agile).

challenge_identified: Front-End team produces lot of bugs (defects) inside a sprint cycle.
related_quote: We are in a loop of pointing out the issues -> Team records it -> on next sprint, same issues -> Retrospective [...]

challenge_identified: Daily Scrum meetings often exceed 15 minutes, leading to frequent interruptions.
related_quote: This results in the Daily Scrum meeting going beyond 15-min. almost daily. [...] My Query: Is it the responsibility of the Scrum master to intervene and interrupt a team member when he/she starts talking about points beyond those 3 that I mentioned? [...] Is this OK as per Agile?

challenge_identified: Longer response times and reduced effectiveness due to remote work.
related_quote: They are taking longer and are less effective now we are working from home because of COVID

challenge_identified: Lag in delivery causing delays and emergency fixes
related_quote: Standard story, I guess, ship deliverable's and then nothing for a week, and then there are 'emergency' fixes needed overnight. If the work needs to get done, it'll get done, but how does one formally delay dates when the lag is the customer's fault?

challenge_identified: Software development team does NOT report to PM, but PM is responsible for hitting milestone dates and not exceeding budget
related_quote: Software development team does NOT report to PM, but PM is responsible for hitting milestone dates and not exceeding budget

challenge_identified: Difficulties adapting to a new role and overcoming team resistance to stand-ups
related_quote: I am a brand new scrum master, and my team almost refuses to do the stand-up, what do I do?

challenge_identified: Commitment mismatch between planned and completed story points leading to difficulties in managing the project.
related_quote: The problem I am facing at the moment, is that we commit to (For arguments sake) 100 story points, but only complete 20. [...] This becomes a nightmare as trying to manage the client becomes almost impossible. [...] Now the logical thing to say here is that we are over committing each sprint or underestimating stories which is very possible.

challenge_identified: Concerns regarding a specific concern raised by two team members, which affects their mental state but not directly.
related_quote: I've been working in a particular Agile/Scrum team for about 5 months now, and I am conscious of a specific concern (that's been mentioned to me in private) for a few weeks, from 2 different team members (out of 8). [...] The concern doesn't affect me directly but I recognize that it does affect those team members and it is legitimate, not just a 'gripe'.

challenge_identified: Difficulties managing a highly productive team member who often misses deadlines and tasks.
related_quote: How to efficiently manage a Scrum team when one member is much less productive?

challenge_identified: Difficulties engaging team members and encouraging active participation in retrospective meetings.
related_quote: My boss missed two retrospectives because he had other meetings and I started making progress. [...] There was more engagement, and one junior team member who had been silent in previous Sprint Retrospectives talked at length about an action item she wanted added.

challenge_identified: Difficulties communicating reasons for unfeasible projects
related_quote: When presented with a new project, which I can rather quickly dismiss as unfeasible (we're talking extremely unrealistic projects), I have trouble communicating why it cannot be done. [...] I argue the technical complexity and the requirements versus the time and price scope.

challenge_identified: Difficulties influencing a scrum team to embrace video conferencing during meetings.
related_quote: How to influence a scrum team to do something you as a SM feel is beneficial to the team. [...] I was thinking of talking individually to each and explain why seeing their faces is important for me to do my job ie is it reasonable given the situation to ask scrum team members to turn on their cameras during ceremonies?

challenge_identified: Non-programming activities cost money and clients dislike paying for them.
related_quote: On one hand, software development involves a lot of non-programming activities like requirements gathering (including analysis), estimating, planning, testing, tracking work, change request management, and more. [...] On the other hand, our clients don't want to pay for these activities. When a non-programming activity shows up on their invoices, they usually get angry and complain

challenge_identified: Management prohibiting test swarming impacts team morale and productivity.
related_quote: What to do when management prohibits test swarming by development team? [...] The point is, we had been working for weeks on moving from timeboxed mini waterfalls to actual Scrum. [...] Given that the team cannot change its structure in the short term, having developers actively participating on testing meant a great advantage for the whole team and we were actually being able to have shippable versions at the end of each sprint.

challenge_identified: Difficulties expressing opinions and ideas during meetings, feeling ignored and demotivated.
related_quote: Whenever I try to voice my ideas/opinions during meetings they are abandoned/ignored. [... The team is dominated two senior people who just discuss among themselves wok which is to be done while the other two are dormant. [...] That makes the situation very demoralizing.

challenge_identified: Team members feel passive and lack engagement.
related_quote: Only the PO is active - he speaks for 90% of time. [...] When I asked him not to speak first (he is willing to change this state) there was silence and it was really hard to end our meetings with any conclusions. [...] The guys are simply fine with everything

challenge_identified: It's such a confusing prospect for me as a scrum master because since they deliver at pace, they have not vocalized any issues, stakeholders are happy there appears to be no problem but on the other hand it's not really what I would call a scrum team.
related_quote: It's such a confusing prospect for me as a scrum master because since they deliver at pace, they have not vocalized any issues, stakeholders are happy there appears to be no problem but on the other hand it's not really what I would call a scrum team.

challenge_identified: My team hates planning poker (and the length of planning in general), is there a more engaging and/or quicker way to plan a sprint?
related_quote: Are there either more engaging ways to plan a sprint (something other than sitting around a table playing with cards/shouting out t-shirt sizes), or a quicker way to do it?

challenge_identified: Erosion of trust, reduced psychological safety, difficulty maintaining leadership
related_quote: The agile coach wants to "observe" all the ceremonies of my team. He wants to do this to "coach" me. [...] He wants to drop into any ceremony when he has time. [...] I am against this because it erodes trust that I am trying to build with my team. [...] It also reduces psychological safety within my team and it also makes it harder to maintain leadership in my team. [...] I also don't feel it's of any benefit for my team.

challenge_identified: Feeling pressured to maintain a formal tone in meetings while desiring a more relaxed atmosphere.
related_quote: From an empathetic standpoint, if I were in the direct report's position, I would prefer to keep things more casual and not be in a 2-1 situation. [...] I feel like I would close down and not give my actual feedback.

challenge_identified: Difficulty discussing outdated website design and lack of modernization efforts among team members.
related_quote: We have a web site that was written five years ago by people that didn't know what they were. [...] Unfortunately, every time I bring up this subject I am told two things by fellow team members: Shut up, we'll handle this piecemeal as things break. [...] If you have any questions, see point 1.

challenge_identified: I am not in a position to change situation myself; my expressed concerns are either politely ignored or 'we have heard that before'.
related_quote: I am not in a position to change situation myself; my expressed concerns are either politely ignored or 'we have heard that before'.

challenge_identified: Employee feels undervalued and unsupported, leading to potential demotivation and loss of a valued team member.
related_quote: I wasn't going to be to negative on him in my official eval, but to me it is like he is asking to be fired. [...] I've never had anyone rate themselves so negatively. [...] He never asks for help. [...] He does his best to help everyone. [...] Now it seems unavoidable to come down on him and start being more hands on. [...] How can this be handled without demoralizing the employee even more and without losing a valuable team member that everyone loves?

challenge_identified: Product Owners face challenges in aligning their strategic goals with the company’s vision.
related_quote: What does a Product Owner do if they disagree with the CEO's direction on product strategy?

challenge_identified: Feeling fed up with silly questions and lack of adherence to Scrum values
related_quote: Frankly speaking, the whole team is quite fed up with all these silly questions, and all of us wonder whether we are actually following Scrum values, or instead just maintaining JIRA to show PMO that we are all following Scrum and have a near-to-perfect burndown chart.

challenge_identified: Difficulties getting dev teams to embrace Test Driven Development (TDD) and Continuous Integration (CI)
related_quote: How to get a dev team to use TDD and CI? [...] I'm a software development program manager who has experienced many of the benefits of test-driven development and continuous integration. [...] But some of the dev teams I work with don't follow these practices.

challenge_identified: Struggles with delegation and task management as a project manager
related_quote: One of my weaknesses as a PM is not being able to delegate and manage work assigned to others.

challenge_identified: Difficulties holding project leaders accountable without offending them
related_quote: how do I hold a project leaders' feet to the fire without offending? [...] I have been bitten in the past for being too honest with my feelings when project leaders drop the ball (like not recording requirements changes and then having to discuss the same things multiple times or helping write extremely poor requirements in the first place).

challenge_identified: Laying down rules and procedures causing confusion and inefficiency.
related_quote: As a Tech Lead within a "Scrum" team, I am annoying regarding some laid rules. [...] It is established that only Product Owner, Business Analyst and Scrum Master (and eventually Product Manager) take part of the user stories prioritization process (backlog prioritization) [...] I notice that their decision is often based on wrong assumptions and false hypothesis; leading to a sorting readjustment, therefore wasting anybody's time.

challenge_identified: A team member with good technical skills but weak in other areas struggles with documentation because it is difficult for him and he avoids doing it.
related_quote: How to approach this: A team member with Good technical skills but weak in other areas [...] I spoke with him and he says that documenting something is hard for him, and he doesn't like to do this so he postpones it to the end of the sprint and rarely does it.

challenge_identified: Product Owners and team members frequently discuss unrelated topics during the Daily Scrum, leading to inefficiency and lack of focus.
related_quote: They tend to go off topic, instead of focusing on what I did, what I am going to do and impediments. [...]The Product Owner talks and asks them for 'updates' during or directly after the Daily Scrum [...] They often don't know what they are going to do [...] i.e. they can't answer the question during the Daily Scrum, so they say they are going to decide after the Daily Scrum what to do.

challenge_identified: Project timeline uncertainty leading to potential delays and missed deadlines.
related_quote: We have got the situation when our FP project is running over the time without visible end date. The initial agreed scope is accomplished so we would like to release the team.

challenge_identified: Constant interruptions from outside the team make it difficult to focus on tasks.
related_quote: How do you deal with constant interruptions coming from outside the team?

challenge_identified: Difficulties speaking up about delays in sprint planning meetings
related_quote: As a Scrum Master, how to speak to a developer about his delays?

challenge_identified: Problem (In my view, many team members do not agree to this): What happens in absence of a Product owner is When we go to planning meetings, we first start giving priority to the tasks as a team based on that one-liner task descriptions which in some cases are explained
related_quote: Problem (In my view, many team members do not agree to this): What happens in absence of a Product owner is When we go to planning meetings, we first start giving priority to the tasks as a team based on that one-liner task descriptions which in some cases are explained

challenge_identified: Collisions between Daily Scrum meetings and other daily activities leading to missed meetings.
related_quote: However, such meetings sometimes collide with the Daily Scrum. Not often enough to actually change the daily time (or place), but often enough for me, because many times people tend to forget about this and miss one or another meeting.

challenge_identified: Difficulties managing a team member who avoids participating in retrospectives
related_quote: How to manage a situation when one team member doesn't want to participate in retrospectives?

challenge_identified: Some people are very comfortable to talk and share ideas/knowledge in order to help the team to define tasks for our stories, but some people (usually more than half of the team) simply left the meeting without even speak out once.
related_quote: Some people are very comfortable to talk and share ideas/knowledge in order to help the team to define tasks for our stories, but some people (usually more than half of the team) simply left the meeting without even speak out once.

challenge_identified: Struggling to obtain support from a reluctant experienced engineer
related_quote: I have one expert engineer on our team but he really is busy and very begrudgingly responds when asked things. [...] I try to keep my questions to 1 every 3 days, so I group them. [...] When I asked how to scope this, or in general where to start he just said open the code base and determine the hours for the project.

challenge_identified: When is it appropriate for a Scrum Master to bring these views to the attention of management or do I just continue trying to get the team to evolve and deal with this on their own?
related_quote: when (if ever) is it appropriate for a Scrum Master to bring these views to the attention of management or do I just continue trying to get the team to evolve and deal with this on their own?

challenge_identified: Product owner struggles with tracking and managing sprint commitments, leading to missed goals.
related_quote: I am on the product side and have an agile team that has missed every sprint commitment through the first 4 by between 40 and 60% measured by points. [...] The team continues to say, that it is OK and that the stories were mostly done and they'll just split and move stories forward.

challenge_identified: UI designers lack necessary skills and knowledge to effectively communicate their design requirements to both business and development teams.
related_quote: What should a UI designer know and how to deliver this knowledge to him? [...] The problem is that she is neither into business nor into development, so her designs are quite typical: as beautiful as useless and/or difficult to implement.

challenge_identified: Multiple follow-ups fail to complete defined tasks within the given timeline.
related_quote: However, in spite of multiple follow-ups, they fail to complete the defined tasks in the given timeline.

challenge_identified: Difficulties in managing client meetings and handling client requests
related_quote: However, some less IT-savvy clients almost always insist to have face-to-face meet-ups for project review. [...] The in-house account manager will then have to go down but most of the time he/she does not possess the technical knowledge to handle some of the client's requests.

challenge_identified: Frustration with jam sessions wasting valuable project time
related_quote: They can waste hours of the day with these 20–30-minute, non-project related jam sessions. [...] Since I am PM on many of their projects, I get frustrated with the lack of focus

challenge_identified: A team member is taking too much time at Daily Scrums. What is the best way to resolve this issue as a Scrum Master?
related_quote: A team member is taking too much time at Daily Scrums. What is the best way to resolve this issue as a Scrum Master?

challenge_identified: Developers are required to enter their work hours in Project Server by time-sheets. However, they are not happy to complete their time sheets.
related_quote: Developers are required to enter their work hours in Project Server by time-sheets. However, they are not happy to complete their time sheets.

challenge_identified: Product UI design complexity due to direct input from the PO.
related_quote: Should the Product Owner dictate what info the UI needs to display? [...] Very often, the PO suggests, in detail, how a user interface of our software should be, such as what type of information is to be displayed in the user interface (e.g. "I'd like to see a date in list" or "I'd like to see so and so in this view"). [...] Often the PO has asked the customer, or knows already, but the product UI ends up being quite cluttered and complex.

challenge_identified: Pain persists despite medical evidence, yet team pressures prevent sitting.
related_quote: I'm experiencing persisting pain in the back, and it's medically proved that it's present and that it will never improve but only degrade. [...] Yet when I'm looking for a chair, my team (both the PM and my colleagues) insists I keep standing up, and the social pressure makes it hard for me to sit.

challenge_identified: My team has certain issues which I have already determined, namely: They are not pairing enough [...]. Their cycle time is very high; They break timeboxed tasks; The team is not splitting QA work equally, they assume the QA guy needs to do it all.
related_quote: How to make a retrospective focus on certain issues which I have already identified [...] My team has certain issues which I have already determined, namely: They are not pairing enough [...] Their cycle time is very high; They break timeboxed tasks; The team is not splitting QA work equally, they assume the QA guy needs to do it all

challenge_identified: People trying to add and change scope at the worst possible time in the project life cycle
related_quote: How do you handle people who obliviously try to add and change scope at the worst possible time in the project life cycle?

challenge_identified: Clients assume certain features are included in the system without explicit specification.
related_quote: Other change requests are what clients claim they 'just assumed would be part of the system' even if not explicitly spelled out in the spec. [...] we need to come up with a better way to both internally identify whether work being requested is 'out of spec', and be able to communicate this to a client in such a manner that they can understand why what they are asking for is 'extra' work.

challenge_identified: Difficulties convincing peers and management to add comments to source code commits.
related_quote: How do I convince my fellow devs to WANT to add comments to source code commits? [...] Even if I can't convince my fellow devs, how can I convince management that it's the right thing to do for the business?

challenge_identified: Difficulties convincing leadership to switch from SOAP to REST APIs
related_quote: How do I convince my boss to use REST over SOAP?

challenge_identified: Should the developer shoot down the idea because it adds unimaginable complexity and security vulnerabilities, or should he do what's asked?
related_quote: Should the developer shoot down the idea because it adds unimaginable complexity and security vulnerabilities, or should he do what's asked?

challenge_identified: Code formatting and organization issues affecting work and productivity
related_quote: Basically, I hate the code they write. It's badly formatted/organized, and sometimes is radically different from the rest of the project. [...] I feel very upset when they add their spaghetti to my piece of art and it affects my mood at work and my productivity.

challenge_identified: Morning Standup meetings often lead to conflicts or disagreements among team members.
related_quote: What should we do to make Morning Standup meeting better and effective [...] This meeting may sound good and process oriented, but sometimes if there is a show down then it ruins the morning mood or if there is a difference in opinion then again it is not a good thing to start with.

challenge_identified: Should everyone attend the retrospective meeting?
related_quote: Who should be the participants in retrospective? [...] Will it be only the team members OR should the Product manager, all the stakeholders and dependent teams also participate. [...] I am of the opinion that only the team should meet to discuss in retrospectives, else all team members will not open up. [...] But one of the managers insists that all the stakeholders should also be included.

challenge_identified: Programmers fixating on trivialities and not contributing meaningfully to design
related_quote: So how do you educate other programmers enough that they stop fixating on trivialities and can meaningfully contribute to the design?

challenge_identified: Struggling to align release dates with technical feasibility and product requirements.
related_quote: I seem to be repeatedly stuck in a situation where release dates are set not based on anything technical, but because someone in Sales has committed to a customer by then

challenge_identified: Regretting lack of business education
related_quote: I am starting to regret not taking any business classes in college because I don't understand the first thing about running a business so I don't always understand what people are wanting, the best I can do is 'think through it'.

challenge_identified: Feeling trapped and unwilling to share problems due to fear of looking incompetent.
related_quote: I feel like once I am stuck on a problem, I find myself closing up, unable to voice it, as if asking for help is a sign of incompetency.

challenge_identified: Product Owner complains about having too much outside noise, especially stakeholder feature requests, and struggles focusing on sprint realization.
related_quote: The Product Owner complains about having too much outside noise (mainly stakeholders feature requests), and he can't focus on the sprint realization. [...] We agree that we should try to educate people on our process implications (sprint durations and product backlog), to reduce the noise.

challenge_identified: Dealing with potential loss of work due to bugs or arguments with the boss.
related_quote: So, what kind of argument should I make to my boss? [...] Or is he right, and there could be a danger of losing all our work from some weird bug?

challenge_identified: Team members express dissatisfaction with lack of involvement in design decisions and feel undervalued.
related_quote: What about a member of the team working above and beyond the expectations put forth during planning and execution of sprints? [...] Other developers complain about not being involved in design decisions related to these stories, since the work was done out of band.

challenge_identified: Rules that programmers must follow frequently lead to confusion and inefficiency.
related_quote: There are several rules I must keep asking programmers to follow very often. [...] They write code and, if it works, job is just done, for them.

challenge_identified: Feeling overwhelmed by repetitive tasks and lack of creativity from team members.
related_quote: Problem is, I end up spending a lot of my day just checking what they did, and fixing bugs and cases they didn't think of. [...] They're not thinkers, they do exactly what I tell them, and nothing more or less. Most of the time, I feel like it would just be easier to do it myself. [...] I am having difficulties communicating with the vendors exactly what is expected of them.

challenge_identified: Constant interruptions and distractions caused by a colleague who frequently requests working code.
related_quote: I have a strange situation at work, where a colleague of mine often asks me and other co-workers for working code. [...] I would like to help him, but this constant request of trivial snippets interrupts my thoughts and sometimes makes it hard to concentrate.

challenge_identified: Believing unconventional approaches to web app dev are unusual and questioning acceptance of critique from colleague
related_quote: My fellow programmer and I both believe that this is a strange and uncommon approach to web application development. Both of us have been in this business for over five years and neither of us have seen this approach. [...] My question is this, how would we approach our co-worker and voice our concerns and what should we do if he does not want to accept the critic?

challenge_identified: Difficulties accepting suggestions and investing too much in existing algorithms, leading to reluctance and fear of perceived incompetence.
related_quote: But still, they are sometimes reluctant to accept my suggestion, partly because they have invested so much in their own algorithm, or partly because of the fear that using a new method would entail more learning time and make them appear to the management as if they are going nowhere.

challenge_identified: Project managers often experience frustration and stress when their tasks take longer than expected.
related_quote: How to handle project manager getting upset when tasks spend multiple days in development?

challenge_identified: Dealing with business expectations and threats to the project.
related_quote: As senior dev, I quickly realized that the functional and technical spec. contained some huge gaps and uncertainties. [...] As first and foremost a developer, I found this a very stressful and difficult situation. [...] The "business" accused IT of being incompetent and being the messenger, I received a few "bullets". [...] The customer threatened to cancel the account.

challenge_identified: Minimizing pushback and resistance from developers while introducing standards and process improvements.
related_quote: What is the best way to introduce standards and process improvements so that the degree of push back and resistance from developers is minimized?

challenge_identified: Struggling to find a balance between adapting to current team practices and maintaining high-quality standards.
related_quote: I've only been on this team for a week, but I see so many issues with their codebase that I imagine I'll be spending more time fighting with what's already written to do things in 'their way' than I would if I were working on a project that, for example, followed more widely accepted coding standards, architecture patterns, and best practices. [...] How do I (tactfully) tell my project manager or lead developer that the project's codebase needs serious work?

challenge_identified: Team members are annoyed and bored during Scrum events, and one individual is verbally expressing dissatisfaction.
related_quote: During Retrospective I can just feel that they want to say 'Stop doing Scrum'. One person does, but the others are silent and I have to deal with this every time.

challenge_identified: Difficulties reconciling differing attitudes towards development among team members, leading to repetitive issues and feelings of isolation.
related_quote: Recently we've had people start here who seem to have an attitude toward development/programming which is different from my own and hard for me to reconcile; they extract just enough information to get the task done but not really learn from it. I find myself going over and over the same issues with them. [...] I understand that part of this could be a personality thing, but I feel it's my job to do my best and push them out of the nest while they're under my wing, so to speak.

challenge_identified: Should you verbally express disagreement or just listen?
related_quote: If the reviewer changes your code and goes through it with you line-by-line, what do you do if you disagree? Sometimes you made a decision X and the reviewer changed it to Y, and you had Y in mind but decided not to do it because of the disadvantages, but the reviewer insists that those disadvantages are not important. Should you voice your disagreement, or just let them continue?

challenge_identified: Persistent problems with almost everyone struggling to complete planned work in sprints, leading to large carryover issues.
related_quote: I've been here for a year and we have a very persistent problem: almost everybody can't finish planned work in almost every sprint. We have huge carryovers in every sprint. [...] I tend to vote for larger estimates, but my teammates almost never learn from their past mistakes and persistently vote low in these estimations.

challenge_identified: Long discussions inhibit innovation and decision-making.
related_quote: In a software team, how do you cut through these long discussions without inhibiting innovation, so that a decision can be made and you can get on to solving the real business problems.

challenge_identified: Difficulties soliciting product suggestions and feedback from current clients.
related_quote: Our initial thought was to solicit product suggestions and feedback on mock-ups from certain members of our current clientele who have made multiple feature suggestions in the past. This seemed good in theory,

challenge_identified: Lack of effective communication among developers leading to unclear knowledge sharing and misinterpretation of components or conventions.
related_quote: Recently I've come to understand that a serious problem at my workplace is the lack of communication between developers. [...] Whenever I create some component or a naming convention or whatever... there's no way I can make it known to all the people who might need to know this. [...] At most I tell it in person to the fellow who asked me for it, but that's about it.

challenge_identified: Code smells and bad engineering practices are a real problem.
related_quote: When I look at our code, I see some code smells and bad engineering practices [...] The latter two seem to be a real problem. [...] I want to convince my teammates to use smaller classes and methods. [...] But should I do that? If yes, then how?

challenge_identified: Should I not care if the code is littered with redundant identifiers and not descriptive names, and continue to use my own way and push for that to become the standard?
related_quote: I've brought up standardizing on the .Net naming conventions in the past and it's just ignored

challenge_identified: Difficulty finding positive recognition at work, feeling undervalued.
related_quote: But I haven't found the company to give any sort of encouragement / appreciation. [...] My seniors who were not involved in the project were given praise, leave, bonuses, etc. [...] Also I was rejected permission to attend an important family function - that makes me ask 'what's the credit I have now'.

challenge_identified: Support staff and marketing departments feel insufficient lead time on new features and bug fixes.
related_quote: However - we're getting flak from our support staff and marketing department that they don't feel that they're getting enough lead time on new features and notifications on bug fixes that could change behavior.

challenge_identified: Concerns about recording issues as defects, potential difficulties in maintaining a positive relationship, and discomfort with the current labeling system.
related_quote: We don't want to come across as being difficult, or taking things too personally here, and we are happy to make all of the changes identified, however we are a bit concerned especially as there is an uneven power balance at play in our relationship. [...] They client's QA lead says that it is standard for them to label every issue they identify as a defect, however, we are a bit uncomfortable about this. Whilst the relationship is good, we don't see a huge problem with this, but we are concerned that, if the relationship suffers in the future, these lists of 'defects' could prove costly for us.

challenge_identified: Design choices often lead to unexpected outcomes and require extensive testing.
related_quote: How to explain why design choices are good?

challenge_identified: Difficulties distinguishing between bugs, tasks, and features in codebase
related_quote: This is where the problems appear. [...] Some say that it is a bug others say it is a task or an extra feature. [...] here start the discussions [...] is this a bug or an extra feature / task?

challenge_identified: Our Scrum master wants us to document our feedback for the iteration before our Retrospective. [...]. Her argument is that she wants us to have time to document our feedback. [...]. Another guy on the team thinks that we should be documenting our feedback during the meeting or right before the meeting.
related_quote: Our Scrum master wants us to document our feedback for the iteration before our Retrospective. [...]. Her argument is that she wants us to have time to document our feedback. [...]. Another guy on the team thinks that we should be documenting our feedback during the meeting or right before the meeting.

challenge_identified: Feeling sad and hurt after receiving harsh criticism
related_quote: For the first time, someone reviews my code and he says it is a mess. [...] I feel so sad and hurt. I really love programming and making them say something like that really hurts me.

challenge_identified: Perception of unfairness and lack of respect towards individual performance
related_quote: He felt that this was a completely unfair feeling.

challenge_identified: Difficulties expressing differing opinions within the team regarding architectural, design, and user interface issues.
related_quote: Our project head is a genius software architect, a gentle and considerate person in general, a geek by nature and delicate by voice. [...] But, at times, we (my teammates and I) differ in opinions -- especially of software architecture issues, system design issues, UI issues, etc., with our leader. [...] When and how (if ever) should we express the difference in opinions?

challenge_identified: Managers and clients have conflicting expectations regarding developer-client interaction.
related_quote: My manager, and old-school mainframe guy, contends that developers should never ever have ANY client contact. [...] His boss, the owner of the company, told him this morning that every single person in the company needs to think of themselves as an extension of the sales department, and needs to be listening all the time for upsell opportunities.

challenge_identified: Feeling pressured to meet unrealistic deadlines due to conflicting demands from both the CTO and internal team dynamics.
related_quote: To do this I have discussion with related team players and calculate a deadline estimation. [...] Sometimes, I got a scared voice from CTO saying that the estimated deadline is too much. [...] Then I have to shorten the deadline. [...] With a shorter deadline, programmers have to work with extra pressure.

challenge_identified: Sometimes he reviews my code without a heads up, making changes right away, and has even broken my work.
related_quote: However, sometimes he reviews my code (he's second in command to our team leader, so that's expected) without a heads up. [...] So sometimes he reviews my changes before they complete the end goal and makes changes right away [...] and has even broken my work once.

challenge_identified: Software development process has not been changed for years leading to product and team failure.
related_quote: I am interested in knowing how to deal with a current software development process that has not been changed for years and will eventually lead to product and team failure.

challenge_identified: Difficulties explaining complex concepts effectively in a team setting
related_quote: However, I have good reason to believe my explanations are not effective. I routinely have hour long discussions trying to explain my code, and on many occasions the conversations have ended with my coworker saying they still don't understand, but they have somewhere else to be (lunch, or home, or a meeting, etc.).

challenge_identified: Product Owner suddenly shoves new stories into the Product Backlog without considering existing backlog items.
related_quote: For the last 3 sprints or so, I've observed our Product Owner suddenly shoving new stories - stories not previously in the Product Backlog -- at the top of the Product Backlog. [...] My question is - how much can the Product Owner really shake up the Product Backlog? [...] Is it really fair game to ignore what's already in the backlog and keep on shoving new stories at the top?

challenge_identified: Difficulties working with a new developer who has become the most active contributor on the project.
related_quote: So now I want to know what to do. I've spent a lot of my time on this project, so I don't want to just get up and leave, but I'm finding it hard to work with this new developer. [...] On the flip side, he is now the #1 committer on the project, with even more commits than the lead developer. [...] I'm not really sure what to do about this. [...] Has anybody else experienced this problem? If so, what did you do?

challenge_identified: Pushback from developer regarding acceptance criteria and edge cases
related_quote: I am getting push back from one of the developers when I reject his stories. [...] He says it's unfair since I don't specify the edge cases and how the program should respond in the acceptance criteria, as he tends to code for only what I describe in the story.

challenge_identified: Difficulties obtaining support from a reluctant experienced engineer
related_quote: I have one expert engineer on our team but he really is busy and very begrudgingly responds when asked things.

challenge_identified: Encouraging team to adopt agile vertical slicing practices
related_quote: How do I get my team to work in agile way and slice things vertically?

challenge_identified: Struggles with delegation and task management as a project manager
related_quote: One of my weaknesses as a PM is not being able to delegate and manage work assigned to others.

challenge_identified: Receiving harsh criticism during a first code review
related_quote: For the first time, someone reviews my code and he says it is a mess. [...] I feel so sad and hurt. I really love programming and making them say something like that really hurts me.
